applied_optimizations.name: Triton fused post-conv kernel (basic)
applied_optimizations.description: Fused min, model bias add, and scaling operations into single Triton kernel. Used BLOCK_SIZE=4096 for optimal performance on MI355X.

applied_optimizations.name: Fold conv bias into fused kernel
applied_optimizations.description: Changed conv to bias=False and folded conv bias into the fused kernel. This eliminates a separate bias addition kernel launch (~0.4ms savings). The conv bias is added BEFORE the min operation to match reference behavior.

attempted_but_failed.name: Per-channel kernel organization
attempted_but_failed.reason: Loop overhead caused significant slowdown (5.08ms vs 3.44ms)

attempted_but_failed.name: Alternative index calculation (v2)
attempted_but_failed.reason: Direct computation was slower than original approach

attempted_but_failed.name: Initial conv bias folding (wrong order)
attempted_but_failed.reason: Added conv_bias AFTER min operation instead of before

notes: Convolution dominates at 85.5% of GPU time - this is MIOpen assembly kernel and cannot be optimized with Triton
BLOCK_SIZE=4096 gives best performance for the fused kernel
Larger BLOCK_SIZE reduces kernel launch overhead
BLOCK_SIZE must be multiple of 64 for AMD wavefront alignment
Original flattened index calculation is faster than alternative approaches
Key optimization: folding conv bias eliminated separate bias kernel (~0.4ms savings)
Further optimization limited by convolution dominance (85.5% of time)

The convolution is already highly optimized by MIOpen
